sendTransportSpend.note: Total local authority expenditure on SEND home-to-school transport, England

eligibleChildren.note: Children with EHCPs entitled to free home-to-school transport, England

averageJourneyTimeSEND.note: Average one-way journey time for SEND pupils receiving LA-funded transport, England

post16SpendPerStudent.note: Average post-16 transport spend per student receiving LA-funded transport, England. Post-16 transport is not free — costs fall on families or discretionary LA budgets.

sources.name: DfE
sources.dataset: SEND Statistics — Education, Health and Care Plans

sources.name: DfE
sources.dataset: Home-to-School Travel and Transport — Statutory Guidance

sources.name: National Audit Office
sources.dataset: Support for Pupils with Special Educational Needs and Disabilities in England

metadata.methodology: SEND transport spending from Section 251 local authority outturn data, covering all 152 upper-tier LAs in England. Eligible children count derived from DfE SEND statistics (children with EHCPs for whom transport is specified in Section H of the plan). Journey times from DfE survey of local authority transport managers. Post-16 spend per student calculated from total post-16 transport expenditure divided by number of students receiving LA-funded transport.
knownIssues: Journey time figures are self-reported by local authorities — methodology varies between LAs
2020 figures depressed by COVID-19 school closures and reduced transport provision
Post-16 transport data excludes students who self-fund or use public transport independently
Some LAs report SEND transport under different budget lines, making cross-authority comparison approximate
